title: Concurrent assertion semantics and vacuity
description: Concurrent assertions evaluate over clocked sequences and can pass vacuously when their precondition never holds.
body: Use when interpreting whether a passing assertion actually proved anything. Do not trust a never-failing assertion, since it may pass only because its antecedent was never triggered. Actions: check the assertion's enable condition, confirm the precondition occurs in stimulus, and inject a violation to prove it can fire. Validation signals include the assertion firing on injected faults and coverage confirming the precondition was reached. Risk boundary: a vacuously passing assertion gives false confidence, so flag any assertion whose precondition coverage is zero.
